title: Flaky, Crumbly British Duck Legs Lasagna
description: A comforting British dish featuring tender duck legs, a tangy pasta-water-emulsion, and a crumbly topping.
instructions: Season the duck legs with salt and pepper, then sear them in a skillet over medium-high heat until browned on both sides.
Transfer the duck legs to a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot, then add the red wine, chicken broth, and mushroom duxelles.
Bring the liquid to a simmer, then cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 30 minutes, or until the duck legs are cooked through and the liquid has thickened slightly.
While the duck is cooking, cook the lasagna noodles according to package instructions.
In a small saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the flour and whisk to combine, then cook for 1-2 minutes, or until the mixture forms a smooth paste.
Slowly add the pasta water to the saucepan, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then cook for 5-7 minutes, or until it thickens to a sauce-like consistency.
To assemble the lasagna, spread a layer of the pasta-water emulsion on the bottom of a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Arrange 4 lasagna noodles on top of the sauce, then add a layer of shredded duck meat, a sprinkle of breadcrumbs, and a sprinkle of grated cheese.
Repeat the layers two more times, finishing with a layer of cheese on top.
Transfer the lasagna to the preheated oven and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the lasagna from the oven and let it cool for 10-15 minutes before slicing and serving.
